A, title V, § 542140 Stat. 807Apr. 30, 2026“Section 16005(c) of title VI of division B of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act () [set out below] shall be applied as if the language read as follows: ‘Subsection (a) shall apply until .’.”Public Law 116–136September 30, 2026Similar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriation acts:, , .Pub. L. 117–328, div. F, title V, § 543136 Stat. 4757Dec. 29, 2022, , .Pub. L. 117–103, div. F, title V, § 541136 Stat. 344Mar. 15, 2022, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–136, div. B, title VI, § 16005134 Stat. 545Mar. 27, 2020“(a)Notwithstanding any other provision of law regarding the licensure of health-care providers, a health-care professional described in subsection (b) may practice the health profession or professions of the health-care professional at any location in any State, the District of Columbia, or Commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States, or any location designated by the Secretary, regardless of where such health-care professional or the patient is located, so long as the practice is within the scope of the authorized Federal duties of such health-care professional.“(b).—DefinitionAs used in this section, the term ‘health-care professional’ means an individual (other than a member of the Coast Guard, a civilian employee of the Coast Guard, member of the Public Health Service who is assigned to the Coast Guard, or an individual with whom the Secretary, pursuant to , has entered into a personal services contract to carry out health care responsibilities of the Secretary at a medical treatment facility of the Coast Guard) who—10 U.S.C. 1091“(1)is—“(A)an employee of the Department of Homeland Security,“(B)a detailee to the Department from another Federal agency,“(C)a personal services contractor of the Department, or“(D)hired under a Contract for Services;“(2)performs health care services as part of duties of the individual in that capacity;“(3)has a current, valid, and unrestricted equivalent license certification that is—“(A)issued by a State, the District of Columbia, or a Commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States; and“(B)for the practice of medicine, osteopathic medicine, dentistry, nursing, emergency medical services, or another health profession; and“(4)is not affirmatively excluded from practice in the licensing or certifying jurisdiction or in any other jurisdiction.“(c)Subsection (a) shall apply during the incident period of the emergency declared by the President on , pursuant to section 501(b) of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act [] ( [5191(b)]), and to any subsequent major declaration under section 401 of such Act [] that supersedes such emergency declaration.”March 13, 2020Pub. L. 93–28842 U.S.C. 5121(b)42 U.S.C. 5170

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Administrator shall enter into a memorandum of understanding with the administrators of the Emergency Management Assistance Compact, State, local, and tribal governments, and organizations that represent emergency response providers, to collaborate on developing standards for deployment capabilities, including for credentialing and typing of incident management personnel, emergency response providers, and other personnel (including temporary personnel) and resources likely needed to respond to natural disasters, acts of terrorism, and other man-made disasters.

children.label: A
children.text: each Federal agency that has responsibilities under the National Response Plan to aid that agency with credentialing and typing incident management personnel, emergency response providers, and other personnel (including temporary personnel) and resources likely needed to respond to a natural disaster, act of terrorism, or other man-made disaster; and

children.label: B
children.text: State, local, and tribal governments, to aid such governments with credentialing and typing of State, local, and tribal incident management personnel, emergency response providers, and other personnel (including temporary personnel) and resources likely needed to respond to a natural disaster, act of terrorism, or other man-made disaster.

children.heading: Assistance
children.label: 2 Assistance
children.text: The Administrator shall provide expertise and technical assistance to aid Federal, State, local, and tribal government agencies with credentialing and typing incident management personnel, emergency response providers, and other personnel (including temporary personnel) and resources likely needed to respond to a natural disaster, act of terrorism, or other man-made disaster.

children.heading: Credentialing and typing of personnel
children.label: c Credentialing and typing of personnel
children.text: Not later than 6 months after receiving the standards provided under subsection (b), each Federal agency with responsibilities under the National Response Plan shall ensure that incident management personnel, emergency response providers, and other personnel (including temporary personnel) and resources likely needed to respond to a natural disaster, act of terrorism, or other manmade disaster are credentialed and typed in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Consultation on health care standards
children.label: d Consultation on health care standards
children.text: In developing standards for credentialing health care professionals under this section, the Administrator shall consult with the Secretary of Health and Human Services.
